Skip to contents
glmassume 0.1.0
  • Reference
    • Diagnosing GLMs with glmassume

Dispersion Test

Source: R/dispersion_test.R
dispersion_test.Rd

Estimates and tests the dispersion parameter for a fitted GLM. For families where the dispersion is fixed at 1 (binomial, Poisson), this tests whether the true dispersion differs from 1. For families with an estimated dispersion (Gamma, inverse Gaussian, Gaussian), this reports the estimated value.

Usage

dispersion_test(model)

Arguments

model

A fitted glm object.

Value

A list of class "dispersion_test" containing:

dispersion

Estimated dispersion parameter.

family

The model family name.

fixed_dispersion

Logical; TRUE if the family assumes dispersion = 1.

statistic

Pearson chi-squared statistic (only for fixed-dispersion families).

df

Residual degrees of freedom.

p_value

P-value testing dispersion = 1 (only for fixed-dispersion families; NA otherwise).

Examples

# Poisson model — tests whether dispersion = 1
counts <- c(18, 17, 15, 20, 10, 20, 25, 13, 12)
outcome <- gl(3, 1, 9)
treatment <- gl(3, 3)
m <- glm(counts ~ outcome + treatment, family = poisson)
dispersion_test(m)
#> Dispersion Diagnostic
#> ---------------------
#> Family: poisson
#> Estimated dispersion: 1.2933
#> Residual df: 4
#> Pearson Chi-sq: 5.1732  p-value: 0.2700
#> => Dispersion > 1 (potential overdispersion)
#>    Consider: quasi-family, negative binomial, or robust standard errors

# Gamma model — reports estimated dispersion
m2 <- glm(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars, family = Gamma(link = "log"))
dispersion_test(m2)
#> Dispersion Diagnostic
#> ---------------------
#> Family: Gamma
#> Estimated dispersion: 0.0133
#> Residual df: 29
#> (Dispersion is estimated, not fixed; no hypothesis test performed)
#>    Review residual plots to assess whether the estimate is stable
